"cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Summary

Envoy crashes if an ext_proc server sends a single gRPC message containing multiple, specially crafted `ProcessingResponse` messages. This can occur when the first response in the batch causes the gRPC stream object to be destroyed, leading to a use-after-free error when Envoy attempts to process subsequent responses in the same gRPC message.

### Details

Envoy crashes when call: callbacks_.onReceiveMessageRaw() in the function AsyncStreamImpl::onData().

The crash happens in this case:
1) ext_proc server packs two unexpected responses into one gRPC message and sends to Envoy.
2) Upon receiving the first `ProcessingResponse`, Envoy call `callbacks_.onReceiveMessageRaw()`.  As the `ProcessingResponse` is unexpected, this function call caused the destruction of this callbacks_ object.
3) In the next iteration of the for loop, Envoy accesses the already freed `callbakcs_` object,  and crashes.

### PoC

1) To reproduce this issue, just configure ext_proc filter under the HTTP filter chain.
2) Run the Envoy binary with trace log enabled.
3) Run the ext_proc server.  Upon receiving a ProcessingRequest, it packs two ProcessingResponse messages in one gRPC message and sends back to Envoy. 
4) From a client VM, sends a HTTP request to Envoy which hits the ext_proc filter.

### Impact

This vulnerability will cause Denial of Service (DoS). The severity is high.  It impacts all supported release versions.  Anyone who uses ext_proc in their deployment might be impacted.
